subject: Joint Distributions
question: Let $X$ and $Y$ be two random variables with joint density function $f_{XY}(x,y) = \begin{cases} 5x^2y & -1 \le x \le 1,\ 0 < y \le |x| \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ Find $f_{X|Y}(x|y)$, the conditional probability density function of $X$ given $Y = y$. Sketch the graph of $f_{X|Y}(x|0.5)$.
answer: For $y \le |x| \le 1$, $0 \le y \le 1$ we have $f_{X|Y}(x|y) = 3\left[\frac{x^2}{1-y^3}\right]$. If $y = 0.5$ then $f_{X|Y}(x|0.5) = \frac{12}{7}x^2$, $0.5 \le |x| \le 1$. The graph of $f_{X|Y}(x|0.5)$ is given below
